title: Treatment of Hepatitis C: DAAs & Protocols
summary: علاج التهاب الكبد C ثورة طبية حقيقية. الادوية المباشرة المضادة للفيروس (DAAs) حلّت محل الانترفيرون والريبافيرين تقريبا بنسب شفاء تتجاوز 95%. تنقسم DAAs الى ثلاث مجموعات حسب هدفها: مثبطات البروتياز NS3/NS4A (previr-)، مثبطات NS5A (asvir-)، ومثبطات NS5B البوليميراز (buvir-). البروتوكول الحالي: Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir لمدة 8 اسابيع او Sofosbuvir/Velpatasvir لمدة 12 اسبوعا.

key_drugs.name: Ribavirin
key_drugs.note: لازال مكونا مهما مع DAAs في حالات معينة حسب النمط الجيني

key_drugs.name: Interferon-alpha-2a
key_drugs.note: لم يعد موصى به في المبادئ التوجيهية الحديثة

key_drugs.name: Glecaprevir
key_drugs.note: يُدمج مع Pibrentasvir - 8 اسابيع - يغطي كل الانماط الجينية

mnemonics.title: PAB = مجموعات الDAAs الثلاث
mnemonics.body:
P = Protease (NS3/NS4A) = -previr
A = Assembly (NS5A) = -asvir
B = polyBuverase (NS5B) = -buvir
كل مجموعة تضرب الفيروس في مرحلة مختلفة!

mnemonics.title: قصة الHCV: ثلاثة حراس
mnemonics.body: الفيروس لينتج نسخا جديدة يحتاج ثلاثة حراس: الحارس الاول NS3 يقص البروتينات (-previr يسجنه). الثاني NS5A يجمع النسخ (-asvir يقيده). الثالث NS5B ينسخ الRNA (-buvir يعميه). اقفل الثلاثة = لا فيروس.

mnemonics.title: 8 مقابل 12 اسبوع
mnemonics.body: تخيّل ساعتين: الاولى تدور 8 ساعات (Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir) والثانية 12 ساعة (Sofosbuvir/Velpatasvir). كلاهما تصل الى نفس النقطة: >95% شفاء لكل الانماط الجينية.

mnemonics.title: CYP = تفاعلات DAAs الدوائية
mnemonics.body:
-previr: يثبط CYP3A = تفاعلات دوائية خطيرة
-asvir: يثبط P-glycoprotein = تفاعلات دوائية
-buvir: قليل التفاعلات = الاامن منها

flashcards.front: What is the main difference between old and modern HCV treatment?
flashcards.back: Old: Interferon + Ribavirin (lower cure rates, severe side effects). Modern: DAAs without interferon, cure rates >95%, better tolerability.

flashcards.front: What is the function of NS3/NS4A Protease in HCV?
flashcards.back: An essential enzyme that cleaves the viral polyprotein into functional proteins necessary for viral replication.

flashcards.front: What is the distinctive suffix of NS3/NS4A Protease inhibitors?
flashcards.back: -previr (e.g., Glecaprevir, Simeprevir, Grazoprevir)

flashcards.front: What is the main drug interaction of NS3/NS4A inhibitors (-previr)?
flashcards.back: They inhibit CYP3A, leading to potential interactions with drugs metabolized by this enzyme.

flashcards.front: What is the function of NS5A in HCV?
flashcards.back: An essential protein for HCV RNA replication and assembly of viral particles.

flashcards.front: What is the distinctive suffix of NS5A inhibitors?
flashcards.back: -asvir (e.g., Pibrentasvir, Velpatasvir, Ledipasvir)

flashcards.front: What is the drug interaction of NS5A inhibitors (-asvir)?
flashcards.back: They inhibit P-glycoprotein (P-gp), altering the distribution of many drugs and causing significant drug interactions.

flashcards.front: What is the function of NS5B in HCV?
flashcards.back: It is the sole RNA Polymerase of the virus, responsible for copying the viral RNA.

flashcards.front: What is the distinctive suffix of NS5B Polymerase inhibitors?
flashcards.back: -buvir (e.g., Sofosbuvir, Dasabuvir)

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of NS5B inhibitors regarding side effects?
flashcards.back: Well tolerated with fewer side effects compared to protease inhibitors and NS5A inhibitors.

flashcards.front: What is the currently recommended first-line protocol for HCV treatment?
flashcards.back: Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir for 8 weeks — effective against all genotypes.

flashcards.front: What is the second recommended protocol for HCV treatment?
flashcards.back: Sofosbuvir/Velpatasvir for 12 weeks — effective against all genotypes.

flashcards.front: What is the cure rate with modern HCV protocols?
flashcards.back: Greater than 95% (Cure rate >95%) with coverage of all genotypes (Pangenotypic).

flashcards.front: Is Ribavirin still used in HCV treatment?
flashcards.back: Yes, it remains an important component with some DAAs in certain cases depending on genotype and disease status, but it is not required in first-line protocols.

flashcards.front: Why is Interferon no longer recommended for HCV treatment?
flashcards.back: Because of the availability of highly effective DAAs (>95%) with better tolerability. Interferon has severe side effects and lower success rates.

flashcards.front: What are the side effects of NS3/NS4A inhibitors?
flashcards.back: Rash, pruritus, nausea, fatigue, and anemia.

flashcards.front: What is meant by Pangenotypic in HCV treatment?
flashcards.back: It means the drug is effective against all HCV genotypes (1–6) without needing to identify the genotype before treatment.

flashcards.front: What is the modern treatment for Hepatitis C?
flashcards.back: DAAs (Direct-Acting Antivirals) ± Ribavirin; use of Interferon has declined.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of NS3/4A inhibitors ('-previr')?
flashcards.back: They inhibit the viral protease necessary for RNA replication; they inhibit CYP3A (drug interactions).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of NS5A inhibitors?
flashcards.back: They block a protein essential for viral RNA replication and assembly; they inhibit P-gp.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of NS5B inhibitors?
flashcards.back: RNA-dependent RNA polymerase inhibitors ('-buvir').

flashcards.front: What is the role of Ribavirin in HCV treatment?
flashcards.back: It remains an important component with Interferon or DAAs depending on genotype and disease status.

flashcards.front: Why has Interferon use declined in HCV?
flashcards.back: Because DAAs are more effective and have fewer side effects.

flashcards.front: What are the side effects of NS3/4A inhibitors?
flashcards.back: Rash, pruritus, nausea, fatigue, anemia.

flashcards.front: Why are drug interactions common with DAAs?
flashcards.back: Because they are metabolized via CYP450 and inhibit CYP3A and P-glycoprotein.

mcqs.q: What is the distinctive suffix of NS3/NS4A Protease inhibitors in HCV treatment?
mcqs.explanation: -previr is the suffix specific to NS3/NS4A Protease inhibitors such as Glecaprevir and Simeprevir.

mcqs.q: The currently recommended first-line protocol for HCV treatment is:
mcqs.explanation: Current guidelines recommend Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir for 8 weeks as the first-choice option for all genotypes.

mcqs.q: What is the function of NS5B in the HCV life cycle?
options: A protease that cleaves the polyprotein
A protein that assembles viral particles
The sole RNA Polymerase responsible for copying the viral RNA
An envelope protein of the virus
mcqs.explanation: NS5B is the RNA-dependent RNA Polymerase of HCV, exclusively responsible for replicating the viral genome.

mcqs.q: The main drug interaction of NS3/NS4A inhibitors (-previr) occurs via:
mcqs.explanation: NS3/NS4A Protease inhibitors inhibit CYP3A, raising the levels of other drugs metabolized by this enzyme and potentially causing toxicity.

mcqs.q: What is the cure rate with modern DAA protocols?
mcqs.explanation: Modern DAA protocols achieve cure rates >95% with excellent tolerability and coverage of all genotypes.
-asvir is specific to NS5A inhibitors such as Pibrentasvir, Velpatasvir, and Ledipasvir.

mcqs.q: Sofosbuvir belongs to the class of:
mcqs.explanation: Sofosbuvir (-buvir) is an NS5B Polymerase inhibitor, used with Velpatasvir as the second-line protocol for 12 weeks.

mcqs.q: Why was Interferon use discontinued in HCV treatment?
options: It is completely ineffective against HCV
DAAs are available with >95% efficacy and better tolerability
mcqs.explanation: Modern guidelines do not recommend Interferon because DAAs are more effective (>95%) and have fewer side effects.

mcqs.q: Drug interactions of NS5A inhibitors (-asvir) occur via:
mcqs.explanation: NS5A inhibitors inhibit P-glycoprotein, affecting the distribution and absorption of multiple drugs and causing significant drug interactions.

mcqs.q: What is the treatment duration of Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir for uncomplicated cases?
mcqs.explanation: Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir is given for 8 weeks only in treatment-naive patients without cirrhosis.

mcqs.q: A drug ending in '-previr' is an inhibitor of:
The current primary treatment for HCV is:
mcqs.explanation: DAAs are the cornerstone of modern treatment.
